question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted timestamp parameter (e.g., timestamp=eq:0) to force getExchangeRate() to resolve Bound.of() to the genesis-era exchange rate stored in file_data, causing downstream consumers to receive a historically incorrect HBAR/USD rate and miscalculate transaction costs?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit two conflicting timestamp parameters (e.g., timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:Y where X > Y) to Bound.of() to trigger an inverted range that bypasses the primarySortField=false check in Bound constructor, causing fileService.getExchangeRate() to return an exchange rate from an unintended historical slot?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user pass timestamp=lte:1 (near-zero nanosecond timestamp) to getExchangeRate() to force fileService.getExchangeRate() to walk back through the entire file_data history via the retryTemplate fallback loop in FileServiceImpl, leaking the oldest exchange rate record and confirming the genesis-era Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Size(max=2) constraint on the timestamp[] array by submitting exactly two EQ operators (timestamp=eq:T1&timestamp=eq:T2) to getExchangeRate(), causing Bound.of() to rewrite both to LTE and produce an ambiguous upper-bound-only range that resolves to a different historical exchange rate than the user-visible timestamp implies?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a maximum-value timestamp (timestamp=lte:99999999999.999999999) to getExchangeRate() to confirm that the latest exchange rate in file_data is returned, and then use binary-search timestamp probing to enumerate the exact consensus timestamps of every historical exchange rate update in the Hashgraph, reconstructing the full rate-change history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp parameter to getFees() that falls between two consecutive fee schedule updates in file_data, causing feeScheduleMapper.map() to select the wrong historical CurrentAndNextFeeSchedule and return gas estimates that do not correspond to the actual on-chain fee schedule at that consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp range (e.g., timestamp=gt:T1&timestamp=lt:T2) to getFees() where the fee schedule and exchange rate files have different consensus timestamps within the range, causing feeScheduleMapper.map() to pair a fee schedule from one historical epoch with an exchange rate from a different epoch, producing a gas estimate that never existed on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FeeScheduleMapper.getEffectiveFeeSchedule() logic by supplying a timestamp that is exactly at the feeScheduleExpirationTime boundary, causing the mapper to select the nextFeeSchedule instead of the currentFeeSchedule, returning fee data from a future historical slot that misrepresents the actual fees at the queried consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FeeScheduleMapper.getEffectiveExchangeRate() logic by supplying a timestamp just past the currentRateExpirationTime to getFees(), causing the mapper to return the nextRate exchange rate for a historical period when the currentRate was actually in effect, producing a historically inaccurate fee-in-tinybars calculation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate the exact consensus timestamps of every historical fee schedule change by binary-searching the timestamp parameter on getFees() and observing changes in the returned gas values, reconstructing the complete fee schedule history of the Hashgraph without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply timestamp=lte:1 to getFees() to trigger the FileServiceImpl retryTemplate to iterate through up to 10 historical file_data records for both the fee schedule and exchange rate files simultaneously, causing amplified database load and leaking the existence and timestamps of historical system file updates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST an unbounded-size protobuf body to estimateFees() (no @Size or content-length limit enforced at the controller level on the byte[] body) to cause Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(body)) to consume excessive heap memory during deserialization, leading to OOM or service degradation that prevents legitimate fee queries from reflecting the current Hashgraph fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a crafted protobuf body to estimateFees() that encodes a valid Transaction with deeply nested fields, causing Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse() to recurse deeply and exhaust stack space, crashing the service and preventing the fee schedule (which reflects Hashgraph history) from being served?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf body to estimateFees() with mode=STATE and highVolumeThrottle=10000 that encodes a transaction type whose STATE mode reads from live DB stores (e.g., token custom fees), causing the fee estimate to reflect the current Hashgraph state rather than the historical state implied by the fee schedule, producing a misleading fee that does not correspond to any historical consensus moment?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf body to estimateFees() with mode=INTRINSIC that encodes a transaction with a very large number of SignaturePair entries in the sigMap, causing the fee calculator to return an inflated fee estimate that does not match any historical fee schedule entry, misleading integrators about the true historical cost of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf body to estimateFees() that encodes a Transaction with signedTransactionBytes containing random bytes, causing Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse() to succeed (valid outer wrapper) but feeEstimationService.estimateFees() to throw IllegalArgumentException('Unable to parse transaction'), and by varying the body size, enumerate the maximum parseable transaction size accepted by the PBJ parser?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf body to estimateFees() with mode=STATE that encodes a ConsensusSubmitMessage targeting a topic ID that exists in the mirror DB with custom fees, causing the STATE mode to read live custom fee state and return a fee that reflects the current Hashgraph state rather than the historical fee schedule, creating a discrepancy between INTRINSIC and STATE mode responses that leaks information about current on-chain state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a zero-byte body to estimateFees() (body=[] with @NotNull satisfied by empty array) to cause Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(new byte[0])) to return a Transaction.DEFAULT, which then causes feeEstimationService.estimateFees() to throw 'Transaction must contain body bytes or signed transaction bytes', leaking internal error message details about the fee estimation pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger integer overflow in toExtra() via detail.perUnit() * detail.charged() (both are long values with no overflow check) by crafting a transaction body that causes the fee calculator to produce perUnit and charged values whose product overflows long, returning a negative subtotal in the FeeEstimateResponse that misrepresents the historical fee for that transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction body for estimateFees() that causes feeResult.getHighVolumeMultiplier() / HighVolumePricingCalculator.HIGH_VOLUME_MULTIPLIER_SCALE to perform integer division truncation, returning a high_volume_multiplier of 0 when the actual multiplier is between 0 and SCALE, misrepresenting the historical high-volume pricing state of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call getNetworkStake() to observe changes in stake values over time, and by correlating the returned staking_period timestamps with known Hashgraph consensus events, reconstruct the historical staking distribution of the network without any authentication, enabling targeted attacks on validator selection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNetworkStake() always returns the latest network stake (no timestamp parameter) to detect the exact moment a new staking period begins by polling the endpoint, and use the timing of stake changes to infer the consensus timestamp of the most recent NodeStakeUpdateTransaction in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp parameter to getSupply() that falls before the first account_balance record in the DB, causing networkService.getSupply() to return a NetworkSupply with consensusTimestamp=0L, which then passes the consensusTimestamp==0 check and throws EntityNotFoundException, leaking that no supply data exists before that timestamp and confirming the genesis block timestamp of the Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two timestamp parameters to getSupply() (e.g., timestamp=gte:T1&timestamp=lte:T2) to access historical supply snapshots from the account_balance table, and by iterating over different timestamp ranges, reconstruct the complete historical released supply curve of the Hashgraph, revealing when large HBAR unlocking events occurred?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getFirstDayOfMonth() optimization in NetworkServiceImpl.getSupply() by supplying a timestamp at a month boundary to cause the optimalLowerBound calculation to select a different month's balance snapshot, returning a supply value that does not correspond to the exact queried timestamp but to the first day of the prior month, misrepresenting the historical supply at that consensus moment?
